findings.title: CWE-78: CPG taint path — PGP_EMAIL = os.getenv('PGP_EMAIL') → subprocess.run(['cp', '-r', dist_path, artifacts_path])

findings.suggestion: Pass a list to subprocess (no shell=True): `subprocess.run(['cmd', arg])`

findings.title: CWE-22: Path traversal in do_checksum() via os.path.join()
findings.description: `do_checksum()` passes `release_file` (possibly user-controlled or DB-sourced) to `os.path.join()` at L90 without sanitization. `../` sequences can escape the intended directory.
findings.suggestion: Sanitize: `from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename; safe = secure_filename(filename)`. Verify resolved path: `assert os.path.realpath(p).startswith(BASE_DIR)`.
findings.auto_fix:
BEFORE: with open(os.path.join(dist_path, release_file), "rb") as f:
AFTER:      safe_path = Path(BASE_DIR, os).resolve()
            assert str(safe_path).startswith(str(BASE_DIR))
            with open(safe_path) as f:

rule.remediation: Normalize against a safe root and reject any resolved path that escapes it.

findings.title: CWE-862: Missing authentication on read_items() — state-mutating route with no authentication
findings.description: `read_items()` is a route handler with no authentication decorator. Any unauthenticated caller can reach this endpoint. Missing: `@login_required` or equivalent.
findings.suggestion: Add `@login_required` above `@app.route`. For admin routes, also verify elevated role.

trace.label: state mutation in route body reachable without auth

rule.title: Python route missing authentication
rule.summary: Flags Flask/FastAPI-style routes with risky behavior and no auth guard.
rule.remediation: Add `@login_required` or equivalent auth middleware before route execution.
known_limitations: Heuristics are strongest on common Flask/FastAPI and Express/Router-style patterns.

findings.title: CWE-307: No rate limiting on authentication route at line 13
findings.description: Authentication route at L13 has no rate-limiting decorator or middleware in scope: `@app.post("/login/")`. An attacker can brute-force credentials, OTPs, or tokens.
findings.suggestion: Add rate limiting: `flask-limiter` for Flask (`@limiter.limit('5/minute')`), `slowapi` for FastAPI, or a middleware-level rate limiter.

rule.title: Python auth/sensitive route missing rate limiting
rule.summary: Flags Flask/FastAPI/Django authentication or sensitive routes (login, MFA, password-reset, token-refresh) where no rate-limiting library is detected in file scope, enabling brute-force attacks.
rule.remediation: Use `flask-limiter` (`@limiter.limit('5/minute')`), `slowapi` for FastAPI, `django-ratelimit`, or a reverse-proxy rate limiter in front of sensitive endpoints.

findings.title: CWE-798: Hardcoded Secret key at line 16
findings.description: A Secret key is hardcoded in source code at L16. This is visible in version control. Rotate this credential immediately.
findings.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secrets manager (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, .env excluded from git).

rule.title: Python hardcoded credential
rule.summary: Flags likely credentials, API keys, or signing secrets committed directly to source.
rule.remediation: Move secrets to environment variables or a secrets manager and rotate any exposed values.

findings.title: CWE-798: Hardcoded JWT signing secret in create_access_token()
findings.description: `create_access_token()` signs a JWT at L103 with a hardcoded secret (`09d25e09...`). Anyone with source access can forge valid tokens.
findings.suggestion: Load the signing key from an environment variable or secrets manager: `key = os.environ['JWT_SECRET']`. Use RS256 with a private key for better security.
findings.auto_fix:
BEFORE: encoded_jwt = jwt.encode(to_encode, SECRET_KEY, algorithm=ALGORITHM)
AFTER:      encoded_jwt = os.environ["encoded_jwt"]

rule.remediation: Ensure all paths return an explicit value or annotate the optional return contract clearly.
